notes: FROZEN / daemon=incapable. Asserts the CORRECT unblock-time behavior, NOT the current (broken) one: parent ready->working at spawn, a Gemini child session appears with ParentSessionID=parent.session_id, parent held working across the child's lifetime, parent working->ready after the child reports back. This is unobservable today — Gemini runs the subagent IN-PROCESS (no separate gemini process to match) and the gemini-cli adapter sets no ParentSessionID (parser.go). The child artifact Gemini writes (chats/<parentSessionId>/<agentId>.jsonl) is deleted on completion (deleteSubagentSessionDirAndArtifactsAsync) and, even transiently, misses deriveParentSession's path-pattern (parent-dir base is the parent UUID, not the literal 'subagents'), so no parent_linked ever fires. known_failing=true: a recording WILL fail these phases until a gemini parent-link adapter rule lands AND the child's transcript is durable under a tailed root. Observations: a Gemini recording does carry a model + per-message tokens (parser parseAssistant/applyTokens), so model/cost/tokens would be non-zero for the parent turn; agent=gemini-cli.

text: Parent Gemini session appears in ready
User prompt drives parent ready -> working (spec bullet 1)
Subagent session materializes (UNBLOCK-TIME: durable child transcript under a tailed root) — a new SessionState for the child
Detector emits parent_linked binding child.session_id -> parent.session_id (spec bullet 2: ParentSessionID = parent.session_id) — UNBLOCK-TIME, requires a gemini adapter parent-link rule

invariants: no state_transition to ready for parent
no state_transition to waiting for parent
text: Parent stays working while the synchronous subagent is alive (spec bullet 4)
After the subagent reports back, the parent's turn ends: working -> ready (spec bullet 5)
